title: Large-Scale Fluorescence-Based Mammalian Cell Reporter Assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6vsf9","text":"Where a scalable fluorescence-based reporter assay in HEK293 cells (using the Bxb1 landing-pad system) has been validated, this protocol describes how to perform a high-throughput screen and subsequently generate a variant effect map using TileSeq.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Transfect DNA library into reporter cell line Seed 15M cells per transfection replicate Assume a minimum of 5% integrants, adjusting depending on amount of complexity required for the variant library. 2. Transfect using a ratio of library:Bxb1 recombinase of 10:1 by mass using FuGENE6 Use Opti-MEM reduced-serum medium for the transfection mix Add mixture 30 minutes after plating insulin promoter-GFP reporter cells in antibiotic-free media (DMEM + 10% FBS). 3. Post 24 hours, stop transfection by replacing media with DMEM + 10% FBS + 1X Pen-Strep. 4. Passage cells every 2-3 days to a maximum of 80% confluence to increase viability after sorting.

Large-scale fluorescence-based assay For a 'non-selection' population of cells: sort for single viable cells expressing the integration mCherry marker (+mCh/-BFP) For a 'selection' population of cell: sort the top 10% of GFP-expressing cells to enrich for functional variants. Expand population of cells, growing to 100% confluency in DMEM + 10% FBS + 1X Pen-Strep. Generate pellets of 15M cells for each the select and non-select conditions Purify DNA and amplify the Bxb1 integration site with: 1000ng of DNA used per reaction Q5 Polymerase preferred for high-fidelity 15 reactions per region (per replicate) per condition (selection or non-selection) A total number of 25 PCR cycles Primers A B 1 Ef1a-Bxb1Amp_F1 CAGGTGTCGTGAGAATTAGCC 2 BxbAmp_R1 CACATTGCCAAAAGACGGCA 6. PCRs pooled, and gel purified

Selection of stable integrants via Fluorescence-Activated Cell Sorting (FACS) At day 7 post-transfection, enrich for integrated variants by gating on: Single, viable cells Cells expressing the integration marker mCherry Cells lacking BFP expression 2. Sort for 2 million cells with integration events per regional biological replicate. 3. Expand integrated, sorted cells for 7 days.

Tiling Procedure: Measuring mutations introduced requires accurate detection of very low allele frequencies, which can be challenged by the base-calling error of Illumina or other NGS platforms. However, base-calling errors can be dramatically reduced by sequencing both strands of short template amplicons (~150bp) that ' tile' the complete coding region, and only calling variants that can be detected on both strands. Forward+Reverse Oligos required for this step – custom primers for each gene for X number of tiles %s %s Thermocycler setting: %s %s Analyze 4μl for each PCR reaction on homemade 2% agarose gel. Use 1μl (of 1/10 diluted) directly for indexing.

Pipeline for Functional Score Determination Quantify variant allele frequencies using TileSeq_MutCount: ( https://github.com/RyogaLi/tileseq_mutcount ) Generate scores, and QC analysis, using tileseqMave ( https://github.com/jweile/tileseqMave )
creator: Warren van Loggerenberg
